6.gl: love, i.e. affection or benevolence; specially (plural) a love-feast

9.gl: the sky; by extension, heaven (as the abode of God); by implication, happiness, power, eternity; specially, the Gospel (Christianity)
9.dv: perhaps from the same as G3735 (ὄρος) (through the idea of elevation);

11.gl: a slave (literal or figurative, involuntary or voluntary; frequently, therefore in a qualified sense of subjection or subserviency)

13.gl: properly, royalty, i.e. (abstractly) rule, or (concretely) a realm (literally or figuratively)
the sky; by extension, heaven (as the abode of God); by implication, happiness, power, eternity; specially, the Gospel (Christianity)
13.dv: perhaps from the same as G3735 (ὄρος) (through the idea of elevation);

22.gl: figuratively, a magistrate; by Hebraism, very
22.dv: of uncertain affinity; a deity, especially (with G3588 (ὁ)) the supreme Divinity;

22.gl: the sky; by extension, heaven (as the abode of God); by implication, happiness, power, eternity; specially, the Gospel (Christianity)
22.dv: perhaps from the same as G3735 (ὄρος) (through the idea of elevation);

23.gl: persuasion, i.e. credence; moral conviction (of religious truth, or the truthfulness of God or a religious teacher), especially reliance upon Christ for salvation; abstractly, constancy in such profession; by extension,
decision (subjectively or objectively, for or against); by extension, a tribunal; by implication, justice (especially, divine law)

28.gl: equitable (in character or act); by implication, innocent, holy (absolutely or relatively)

29.gl: equitable (in character or act); by implication, innocent, holy (absolutely or relatively)

30.gl: blood, literally (of men or animals), figuratively (the juice of grapes) or specially (the atoning blood of Christ); by implication, bloodshed, also kindred

35.gl: blood, literally (of men or animals), figuratively (the juice of grapes) or specially (the atoning blood of Christ); by implication, bloodshed, also kindred
equitable (in character or act); by implication, innocent, holy (absolutely or relatively)

39.gl: supreme in authority, i.e. (as noun) controller; by implication, Master (as a respectful title)
